topic.what: Process mining-based severity rating and explanation mechanism for anomaly-based IDS alerts
topic.how: Builds process-based characterization of false-positive flows using Petri nets; rates alerts by cosine similarity between alignments and false-positive process model
topic.why_matters: Addresses black-box trustworthiness gap in deep learning-based IDSs through process-based explanations grounded in packet-level TCP event sequencing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Detection rate for true positive attacks
characteristics.evidence: AE classifier with low severity band threshold
AE classifier with low severity band threshold

characteristics.what_itasures: Proportion of false positives discarded in low/very-low severity bands
characteristics.evidence: AE discarding 8 FPs out of 8 in very-low band

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to separate TP from FP across severity bands
characteristics.evidence: TPs concentrated in high/medium bands; FPs in low/very-low bands
characteristics.context: Consistent across OCSVM, AE, VAE classifiers

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cosine similarity between alert alignments and false-positive characterization
characteristics.evidence: TP flows show low similarity indicating malicious deviation

novelty: Model-agnostic process-based XAI layer for black-box IDS
Severity rating mechanism inversely proportional to false-positive similarity
Petri net-based characterization of TCP event patterns for alarm explanation
Alignment-based conformance checking for network flow deviation detection
open_problems: Protocol-specific misaligned TCP event cost weighting
Benchmarking against SHAP/LIME alarm ranking methods
Generalization across datasets and network protocols
Extension to non-TCP protocols in ISO/OSI stack
